title: Code review fixes — process.exit() inconsistency, duplicate imports in init.js
description: Two issues found in the LINKS-017/018/019 review. Primary: register.js uses process.exit() which bypasses finally blocks and is inconsistent with every other command. Secondary: init.js has two separate import statements from the same module.

features: FIX 1 — src/commands/register.js: replace all process.exit(1) calls with process.exitCode = 1; return; to match the pattern used consistently across every other command in the codebase. There are multiple occurrences — replace all of them. process.exit(0) (if any) should become return; only.
FIX 2 — src/commands/init.js: merge the two separate import statements from '../config.js' into one: import { writeConfig, validateUrl } from '../config.js';

notes: register.js action handler is async — process.exitCode = 1; return; works correctly in async Commander actions. Verify no process.exit() calls remain in register.js after the fix.
